programmes.show_description: 24 Thousand Ball Point Pens. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie always wanted to make money. When he meets Inky Ballantine he gets involved in a financial caper.
1971. Western. Stars Lee Van Cleef, Carroll Baker & Stuart Whitman. A Native American becomes an Army officer tasked with investigating a murder. His only clue is buried in the victim's final words.
1943. Western. Director: George Archainbaud. Hopalong Cassidy (William Boyd) and his sidekicks (Andy Clyde, Jimmy Rogers) expose a scheme to replace a ranch heir with an impostor.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life East of Suez
programmes.show_description: 1966. An intriguing look at how British troops are policing the rivers and jungles of Borneo and a glimpse of some of the problems that have to be overcome.
The Land Beyond. Western starring Wayne Rogers, Richard Eyer & Robert Bray. Luke & Simon foil a gunman who provokes a passenger on their stagecoach run and the vicious gunslinger tracks the stage.
1967. A magnificent look at the 'in' scene of London, with a focus on the fashions of today and all the different shops where you can buy trendy clothes.
1959. Apache Trail. Western with Peter Breck. After his brothers are killed in a shootout, a gunfighter decides to hang up his guns and uphold the law by becoming a lawyer.
1952. Romance. Directed by Henry King & Roy Ward Baker. Stars Gregory Peck & Susan Hayward. A writer reflects on his life as he lies dying from an infection while on safari near Mount Kilimanjaro.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Look at Man
programmes.show_description: 1969. A visit to the Isle of Man to discover why it wants greater independence.
1941. War. Director: Anthony Asquith. Stars Leslie Banks, Alastair Sim & John Mills. Nazi spies learn about a new device created by a British inventor and send an undercover agent to his cottage.
1951. Drama. Director: Jeffrey Dell. Stars Maxwell Reed, Edward Underdown & Natasha Parry. An inspector hunts a killer who is looking for an actress who witnessed him commit murder.
The Queen's Knight. 1956. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. Queen Guinevere is kidnapped by Sir Mordred who demands King Arthur's kingdom in exchange for the queen's life.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life You're Under Inspection
programmes.show_description: 1965. This short film investigates the investigators of all the different aspects of today's world.
1957. Drama. Director: Compton Bennett. Stars Phyllis Kirk, Dan O'Herlihy & Wilfrid Hyde-White. Robberies and murder take place in a quiet town and the investigators have a lot of questions.
1968. A wonderful look at the world of the Chinese who have made Britain their home, and in particular London's 'Chinatown'.
Hidden Scars - Part 1. 1974. Stars Maureen Lipman, Godfrey Quigley & William Mervyn. The bursar of a charitable school is accused of misusing funds though investments and using the money himself.
Hidden Scars - Part 2. 1974. Stars Terrance Hardiman, Stephen Murray & Ann Castle. Cresswell's wife let slip that she advised the defendant to invest in Metalia on behalf of his disabled daughter.
Hidden Scars - Part 3. 1974. Stars Liz Smith, Stefan Gryff & Morris Perry. Fowkes says charitable money could be given to his daughter. His wife said the money was from a generous friend.
2021. Documentary. Director: Clelia Cohen. A portrait of the last cowboy Hollywood Legend, Clint Eastwood. Dive into a 65-year career from 'The Good, The Bad and The Ugly', to 'Cry Macho' in 2021.
Duffy Calls the Tune. 1959. Stars Jack Warner, Polly Adams & Jennifer Wilson. Music lover, Duffy Clayton winds up in a spot of trouble by keeping the wrong company. Can Dixon help his old friend?
The Rendlesham Incident. 1994. Michael Aspel delves into the story of a UFO sighting at a US airbase in Suffolk, which is claimed to have been covered up by the Ministry of Defence.
Snakes and Ladders. 1973. Stars Jennifer Wilson, Julia Goodman & Gabrielle Drake. Carter finally lays out his conditions for the merger. Meanwhile, Ann confronts Brian about his new lady friend.
The Hostage. 1958. Stars Donald Gray, Diana Decker & Dorothy Gordon. Mark Saber feels his duty to act after an unknown woman phones him and threatens to commit suicide.

programmes.show_name: The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club
programmes.show_description: Episode 30. 1975. Lyn Paul sings 'Behind Closed Doors' and 'It Oughta Sell a Million'. Miki and Griff and Broken Hearts also perform.
1987. Thriller. Director: Peter Yates. Stars Cher, Dennis Quaid & Liam Neeson. A homeless man is accused of murder and a public defender is tasked with mounting his legal defence.

programmes.show_name: Let No Man Write My Epitaph
programmes.show_description: 1960. Drama. Directed by Philip Leacock. Stars Burl Ives & Shelley Winters. The residents of a tenement building band together to ensure that a young man does not follow in his father's footsteps.
The Devil's Sweets. 1970. Stars Simon Oates, Maurice Roëves & John Young. A new brand of chocolate has a sinister side-effect. Doomwatch decide to investigate the matter.
1980. Sci-Fi. Two survivors of an alien spaceship crash-land on earth and hold two people hostage. Starring Cameron Mitchell.

programmes.show_name: A Lifetime Ago with The Baim Collection
programmes.show_description: York. 1972. Valentine Dyall tells us about York, an ancient city steeped in history. Whilst its outlook is to the next century, it retains the atmosphere of its heritage.
1963. Who Killed Beau Sparrow? Directed by David McDearmon. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. Burke sees artist Beau Sparrow take a hard dive into a pool, a dive which kills him.
1960. Jeb's Daughter. Stars Michael Ansara, John Anderson & Suzanne Lloyd. Sam tries to help a young woman who lives in the backwoods with her father... who has decided to sell her.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Flying Soldier
programmes.show_description: 1965. A captivating look at how members of the British army are trained to fly helicopters.
1953. Drama. Directed by Ida Lupino. Stars Joan Fontaine, Ida Lupino & Edmund Gwenn. A man secretly married to two women begins to feel the pressure of his deception.
1959. Drama. Director: Montgomery Tully. Stars Ronald Howard, Carol Marsh & Ian Fleming. After being arrested for robbery and murder, Jenson breaks jail to find the real criminals to turn them in.
1939. Drama. Directed by John Cromwell. Stars Carole Lombard, James Stewart & Charles Coburn. After a whirlwind courtship, a young lawyer and his new wife's love is put to the test.
1965. Comedy. A gang of crooks are caught on a big job. 15 years later they find their loot is now buried under a police station. Stars Sidney James, Sylvia Syms, Dick Emery & Joan Sims.
Number Six. 1962. Directed by Robert Tronson. Stars Ivan Desny, Nadja Regin & Joyce Blair. Charles Valentine discovers that he is being followed by a secret agent from Scotland Yard.
Dead or Alive. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf, Archie Duncan. Little John, a serf at Rutland Court, is ordered to capture Robin Hood. Discovering the outlaw's camp, John joins the band.
1970. Comedy. Directed by Ralph Thomas. Stars Leslie Phillips, Harry Secombe & Angela Scoular. A British doctor stows away on a cruise liner in pursuit of the woman he plans to propose to.
The Authentic McCoy. 1961. Stars John Ireland, Bill Nagy, Neil Hallett. When penniless Henry McCoy dies, his lawyer discovers he was once rich and plans to get the cash before his son shows up.
1958. Directed by Kenneth Fairbairn. A driver of a coaching tour accounts a wonderful trip across the country with a group of friendly travellers, with glimpses of Stonehenge and the Lake District.
1951. Drama. Director: Maurice Elvey. Stars David Farrar, Geraldine Fitzgerald & Roland Culver. When a Victorian woman dies, a postmortem reveals that her body contains a fatal dose of arsenic.
Person Unknown. 1962. Stars Brian Blessed, Joseph Brady & Leslie Sands. Barlow investigates a serial child molester while PC's Smith and Weir discover a young girl who has been attacked.
1969. Detective & Mystery Drama. Paid In Full. Stars John Baldwin, Alfred Burke & Peter Cellier. When money goes missing, the finger of suspicion is naturally pointed at the ex-convict.
Caroline Munro opens tonight's show with the final episode of Supernatural: Dorabella, a haunting story of capture and escape.
Dorabella. 1977. Stars Jeremy Clyde, David Robb & Ania Marson. A handsome young man tells the story of his escape from a female vampire in Eastern Europe.
Next up at the Cellar Club, Caroline Munro introduces the classic 1960s French horror film, Les Yeux Sans Le Visage.
1960. Horror. Director: Georges Franju. Stars Pierre Brasseur, Alida Valli & Juliette Mayniel. A surgeon causes an accident that disfigures his daughter so he goes to extreme lengths to fix her.
